query: Attention Is All You Need Vaswani arXiv year
answer: "Attention Is All You Need" was published in 2017 by Ashish Vaswani and colleagues, introducing the Transformer model based solely on attention mechanisms. The paper was first released as an arXiv preprint in July 2016.

results.title: Attention Is All You Need (Vaswani et al., ArXiv 2017)
results.snippet: The idea in this work is to use attention, applied multiple times, to get a network that is fast while still capturing positional information.

results.title: [PDF] Attention is All you Need | Semantic Scholar
results.snippet: A new simple network architecture, the Transformer, based solely on attention mechanisms, dispensing with recurrence and convolutions entirely is proposed, which generalizes well to other tasks by applying it successfully to English constituency parsing both with large and limited training data.Expand. A new architecture to proficiently mines the ability of attention mechanism and stacked recurrent neural networks for neural machine translation (NMT) tasks is proposed.Expand. This paper's simplified architecture consists in the decoder part of a transformer model, based on self-attention, but with locality constraints applied on the attention receptive field, which achieves a new state of the art of 35.7 BLEU on IWSLT'14 German-English.Expand. This paper illustrates the approach to the shared task on similar language translation in the fifth conference on machine translation (WMT-20) with a recurrence based layered encoder-decoder model with the Transformer model that enjoys the benefits of both Recurrent Attention and Transformer.Expand.

These convinced the team that the Transformer is a general-purpose language model, and not just good for translation.[[5]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Attention_Is_All_You_Need#cite_note-:1-5). The team was named Team Transformer.[[9]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Attention_Is_All_You_Need#cite_note-wired-9). One key innovation was the use of an [attention mechanism](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Attention_(machine_learning) "Attention (machine learning)") which used neurons that multiply the outputs of other neurons, so-called _multiplicative units_.[[10]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Attention_Is_All_You_Need#cite_note-11) Neural networks using multiplicative units were later called _sigma-pi networks_[[11]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Attention_Is_All_You_Need#cite_note-PDP-12) or _[higher-order networks](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Higher-order\_neural\_network&action=edit&redlink=1 "Higher-order neural network (page does not exist)")_.[[12]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Attention_Is_All_You_Need#cite_note-13) LSTM became the standard architecture for long sequence modelling until the 2017 publication of transformers. As evidence, reversing the input sentence improved seq2seq translation.[[23]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Attention_Is_All_You_Need#cite_note-25). 2.   **[^](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Attention_Is_All_You_Need#cite_ref-14)**Some architectures, such as RWKV or state space models, avoid the issue. 7.   **[^](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Attention_Is_All_You_Need#cite_ref-7)**Murgia, Madhumita (23 July 2023). **[^](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Attention_Is_All_You_Need#cite_ref-13)**Giles, C. **[^](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Attention_Is_All_You_Need#cite_ref-feldman1982_17-0)**Feldman, Jerome A. **[^](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Attention_Is_All_You_Need#cite_ref-33)**Marche, Stephen (23 August 2024). **[^](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Attention_Is_All_You_Need#cite_ref-35)**Kaiser, Łukasz (19 June 2017).
